L1.area: HTML report generation and rendering layer

L2.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where user-controlled message content is embedded into dynamically generated HTML output without proper encoding, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary scripts that execute in the context of other users' browsers.

L3.area: Core HTML report generator responsible for rendering status messages and action feedback in the monitoring dashboard
L3.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where user-controlled status messages are written into inline JavaScript blocks within dynamically generated HTML monitoring reports. The message content is JavaScript-encoded but not HTML-encoded before being embedded in a script context, allowing script injection when crafted input is supplied.

ground_truth.reason: User-controlled message strings are passed to a JavaScript alert call in generated HTML without HTML encoding, allowing injection of arbitrary script content via crafted input.
